title: Add traceCapable indication
body:
We recently bumped our Verilator build and ran head first into #4462, which adds a tracing method to models even when no tracing is enabled in the build.

In a classic case of Hyrum's Law, [this broke our build](https://xkcd.com/1172/).

We setup our test framework to automagically generate coverage reports, traces, and other test artifacts depending on the capabilites of the given Verilated model, that way the same test can be used to generate various data depending on how the model is generated.

Basically we have something like this:

```cpp
template <typename T>
concept traceable = requires(T a, VerilatedFstC fst) { a.trace(&fst, 1); };

template <typename T>
concept not_traceable = evalable<T> && !traceable<T>;
```

And various functions with different overloads depending on if model is traceable or not (among other things).

I don't have any objection to #4462, but for my own sanity I would appreciate a flag to hard remove the tracing method/restore the old behavior.
